title: phegopteris dryopteris
intro: Phegopteris dryopteris, also known as the Dryopteris root or the Male fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the Dryopteridaceae family. Traditionally, the root of this plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Phegopteris dryopteris include its ability to aid in the treatment of parasitic worm infections, as well as its use as a natural remedy for fever and rheumatism. In herbal preparations, the root of Phegopteris dryopteris is often dried and used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
